$comment: c56 (D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-NULL-FUNCTION-POINTER): a ternary with one arm a function designator (FnSig) and the other a literal-0, assigned to a fn-ptr lvalue. SQLite `w.xSelectCallback = (...) ? 0 : resolveSelectStep` (sqlite3.c:111984) fired S0003 S_TypeMismatch: combineTernary's c49 null-ptr rule matched only TypeKind::Ptr, not FnSig, so `0 : designator` fell through to I32 -> isAssignable(Ptr<FnSig>,I32) failed. FIX (the c49 fn-pointer sibling, both tiers): a FnSig arm opposite a literal-0 decays to Ptr<FnSig> (the designator -> FnSig->Ptr Bitcast, the literal-0 -> null-ptr Cast). VALUE-CORRECT (the picked fn is CALLED; BOTH arm orders, since the fix also cures a latent miscompile in the designator-first order): order1 w.cb=flag?0:add40 (flag==0 -> add40(2)=42); order2 w.cb=t?sub1:0 (t==1 -> sub1(43)=42); return 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert -> the `? 0 : add40` assignment -> S0003. Pure c-subset -> all 4 targets; pe RUNS on Windows.
